items.headline: PLD Athena Phase B receives $35M site work permit
items.summary: A $35 million site preparation project at 5853 Rue Ferrari is cleared to begin, making it by far the largest construction permit issued in this batch. The scale suggests a major development underway in that corridor.

items.headline: Tous Les Jours bakery-café permitted at Evergreen Plaza
items.summary: The Korean bakery chain Tous Les Jours received a permit for a new restaurant location at 2394 Evergreen Pl.

items.headline: City Council updates district office budget policy for transition years
items.summary: The council is proposing changes to City Council Policy 1-18, Section 20, which governs how Office of the Mayor and City Council District Office budgets are handled during transition years. This affects how funding flows to the nine council district offices when leadership changes.

items.headline: Seeds of Peace Monument installed at Guadalupe River Park
items.summary: The city is approving an agreement for a new artwork titled Seeds of Peace, created by artist Mario Chiodo, to be permanently located at Guadalupe River Park Arena Green West. The work commemorates survivors.

items.headline: Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District sets 2026-27 budget
items.summary: The council is taking action on the FY 2026-27 budget and annual assessment for the Tully Road Eastridge Business Improvement District, which manages the commercial area along that corridor.

items.headline: Cupertino General Plan Health and Safety Element updated
items.summary: The City Council considered updates to the Health and Safety Element of the General Plan, which guides land use and development through 2040. The agenda does not provide specifics on what changes were proposed or decided.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This fills the leadership position responsible for day-to-day city operations and implementation of council policy.

items.headline: City updates water shortage response plans
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission will review the draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. These documents set Sunnyvale's strategy for managing water supplies during drought conditions and guide long-term water infrastructure needs.
